mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and the consumer price index indicate strong demand outpacing supply constraints, boosting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive returns, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient manufacturing output that offsets higher borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the employed population and aggregate demand, directly supporting corporate earnings growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1100 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, dragging down economic growth forecasts and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where HOUST drops below 1100.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative and erode purchasing power, prompting market repricing of equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS levels fails to predict returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50% and Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms economic stabilization, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth during recovery periods is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power when volatility indices exceed 25.
